questions.question: Why does attention need positional encoding at all?
options: To speed up the softmax computation

Scaled dot-product attention is permutation-invariant: shuffle the input rows and the output rows shuffle identically — "the cat sat on the mat" and its scrambled version are indistinguishable without injected position
GPUs require sequential indices
It prevents attention scores from overflowing
questions.explanation: Order-blindness is fine for bag-of-words; for language, code, audio, and video — anywhere order carries meaning — it's fatal.

questions.question: What are the three eras of positional encoding?
options: One-hot, binary, and decimal positions

Absolute sinusoidal (Vaswani 2017: add sin/cos of position, extrapolates poorly), RoPE (Su 2021: rotate Q and K by position-proportional angles, dominant in 2026), and ALiBi (Press 2022: per-head linear distance penalties on attention scores, excellent extrapolation)
Learned, frozen, and hybrid embeddings
Char-level, word-level, sentence-level
questions.explanation: Essentially every frontier open model uses RoPE — Llama 2/3/4, Qwen, Mistral, DeepSeek-V3, Kimi. Absolute sinusoidal is historical.

questions.question: What is RoPE's "beautiful trick"?
options: It needs no trigonometry

Rotating Q at position m and K at position n by angles θ_i·pos makes the dot product q'_m·k'_n a function of (m − n) ALONE — relative position emerges in the attention score even though rotations used absolute positions
It adds positions only to values
It learns positions during fine-tuning
questions.explanation: Per-dimension-pair frequencies θ_i = base^(−2i/d_head) with base 10000. Rotation acts on Q and K, never the embeddings or V.

questions.question: How did models like Llama 3 extend context from 8K to 128K without retraining from scratch?
options: By switching to ALiBi mid-training
By scaling RoPE's base frequency — NTK-aware scaling, YaRN, and LongRoPE adjust the rotation frequencies so trained relative-position behavior stretches to longer contexts
By doubling the parameter count
By truncating all inputs to 8K
questions.explanation: Because position lives in a continuous rotation rather than a learned table, the frequency base becomes a tunable extrapolation knob.

questions.question: Why does absolute sinusoidal encoding fail beyond trained lengths?
options: The sine function is undefined past max_len
The model only learned to read phase patterns for positions it saw — nothing taught it what position 2048 means when training covered 0-2047
The PE matrix runs out of memory
Sinusoids repeat exactly at max_len
questions.explanation: ALiBi's distance-penalty approach extrapolates naturally because "further away = more penalty" generalizes to any length — the property that motivated moving beyond additive embeddings.
